agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Subscription billing platform managing lifecycle states (activation, renewal, cancellation) with payment validation, retry logic, tier-specific grace periods, and free trial constraints.","bounded_context":"Subscription Billing","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Subscription","definition":"A recurring billing agreement between a customer and the platform, with a defined billing cycle and tier."},{"term":"Payment Method","definition":"A stored instrument (card, bank account) used to collect subscription fees; has a status of valid or failed."},{"term":"Billing Cycle","definition":"The recurring period (e.g., monthly, annual) that defines when payment is due."},{"term":"Renewal","definition":"The automated process of charging the subscriber's payment method to continue the subscription into the next billing cycle."},{"term":"Grace Period","definition":"A window of time after failed payment during which the subscription remains active before cancellation (applies to Premium tier)."},{"term":"Free Trial","definition":"A time-limited subscription that does not charge the customer, subject to one-per-email constraints."},{"term":"Premium Tier","definition":"A subscription level that receives enhanced cancellation protection via a 14-day grace period."},{"term":"Failed Payment Method","definition":"A payment instrument that has been declined or is otherwise unable to process charges."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A subscription cannot be activated with a failed payment method on file.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state integrity constraint. Activating a subscription with a known-bad payment method would create a fundamentally corrupt system state — a billable agreement with no ability to collect payment. This is not a policy preference; it prevents the system from entering an inconsistent state where an active subscription has no viable funding source. No VP policy change should allow this because it would undermine the entire billing model.","testable_assertion":"Given a customer with only failed payment methods, when they attempt to activate a subscription, then the system must reject the activation and the subscription must not transition to active state."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Subscriptions auto-renew 7 days before the billing cycle ends to allow retry time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 7-day lead time is a business policy decision balancing retry opportunity against customer experience. A product leader could reasonably change this to 5 days, 10 days, or any other window based on payment processor retry success data. The system would not be in a corrupt state if this number changed — it is a tunable operational parameter.","testable_assertion":"Given an active subscription, when the current date is exactly 7 days before the billing cycle end date, then the system must initiate an automatic renewal charge attempt."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"After 3 failed renewal attempts, the subscription is automatically cancelled.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 3-attempt threshold is a business policy governing involuntary churn tolerance. A VP could change this to 2 or 5 attempts based on cost-of-retry analysis, customer retention goals, or payment processor fees. The system's data integrity is not compromised by adjusting this threshold — it is a configurable policy, not a structural constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a non-Premium subscription with 2 prior failed renewal attempts, when the 3rd renewal attempt fails, then the subscription must be automatically transitioned to cancelled status."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancelled subscription cannot be reactivated — customers must create a new subscription.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a state machine integrity constraint. Allowing a cancelled subscription to be reactivated would corrupt the subscription lifecycle by creating ambiguous billing history — which billing cycle does the reactivated subscription resume from? What happens to the gap period? This enforces a clean, unidirectional state transition (cancelled is a terminal state), preserving audit trail integrity and revenue recognition accuracy. Violating this would create data consistency problems across billing, accounting, and reporting systems.","testable_assertion":"Given a subscription in cancelled status, when any attempt is made to transition it to active status, then the system must reject the transition and return an error indicating a new subscription must be created."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Premium tier subscribers get a 14-day grace period before cancellation instead of the 3-strike rule.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Both the 14-day duration and the decision to give Premium tier differentiated treatment are business policy decisions. The grace period length could be changed to 21 days or 10 days based on churn analysis. The tier-differentiation strategy itself is a product decision. Neither the duration nor the tier-specific application represents a structural integrity constraint — the system remains consistent regardless of the specific grace period values.","testable_assertion":"Given a Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal, when the failed payment occurred fewer than 14 days ago, then the subscription must remain active and not be cancelled. When the 14-day grace period elapses without a successful payment, then the subscription must be automatically cancelled."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Free trial subscriptions cannot have more than one active trial per email address at any time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a data consistency and abuse-prevention constraint. Allowing multiple concurrent trials per email would represent a corrupt state — the system would be granting duplicative free access that fundamentally undermines the trial-to-paid conversion model. This is not a tunable threshold (you cannot have '1.5 active trials'); it is a binary uniqueness constraint that preserves system integrity against abuse. Relaxing it would not be a policy tweak but a structural change to the trial model itself.","testable_assertion":"Given an email address that already has an active free trial subscription, when a request is made to create another free trial for the same email, then the system must reject the request and the total count of active trials for that email must remain exactly one."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose only payment method on file has a failed status","when":"The customer attempts to activate a new subscription","then":"The system rejects activation with a clear error message and no subscription is created in active state"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An active subscription with a billing cycle ending in exactly 7 days","when":"The system's renewal scheduler runs","then":"A renewal charge attempt is initiated against the subscriber's payment method"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A standard-tier subscription that has accumulated 3 consecutive failed renewal attempts","when":"The 3rd failure is recorded","then":"The subscription status transitions to cancelled automatically and the customer is notified"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A subscription in cancelled status","when":"Any API call or user action attempts to reactivate it","then":"The system returns an error indicating reactivation is not permitted and instructs the customer to create a new subscription"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Premium tier subscription with a failed renewal attempt within the last 14 days","when":"The grace period evaluation runs","then":"The subscription remains active and is not cancelled until the full 14-day window expires without successful payment"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer email address with an existing active free trial","when":"A second free trial creation is attempted for the same email","then":"The system rejects the second trial and returns an error indicating only one active trial per email is allowed"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer's payment method fails between subscription creation and activation","expected_behavior":"If the payment method transitions to failed status after the subscription is created but before activation completes, activation must still be blocked per BR-1."},{"scenario":"Premium subscriber's payment succeeds on day 13 of the grace period","expected_behavior":"The subscription remains active, the grace period resets, and the successful payment is recorded as the renewal."},{"scenario":"3rd renewal failure and 14-day grace period overlap for a recently-downgraded Premium subscriber","expected_behavior":"The tier at the time of the first failure determines which rule applies. If the subscriber was Premium when the failure sequence began, the 14-day grace period governs."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels and immediately tries to reactivate with a valid payment method","expected_behavior":"Reactivation is rejected regardless of payment method validity. Customer must create a new subscription."},{"scenario":"Free trial user's trial expires and they attempt a new trial with the same email","expected_behavior":"An expired trial is no longer active, so a new trial should be permitted since the one-active-trial constraint is no longer violated."},{"scenario":"Renewal attempt fires but payment processor is unreachable","expected_behavior":"A timeout or network failure should count as a failed attempt toward the 3-strike limit (or be treated as a retriable transient error — this needs clarification)."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct enforcement of these rules directly protects revenue: blocking failed payment activations prevents unbillable subscriptions, retry windows maximize collection rates, and trial abuse prevention protects the conversion funnel.","operational":"Auto-cancellation after failed retries reduces manual intervention. The Premium grace period increases support complexity but reduces high-value churn. The terminal cancellation state simplifies billing system state management.","user":"Customers experience predictable subscription lifecycle behavior. Premium users get enhanced protection. The no-reactivation rule may frustrate customers who accidentally cancel but enforces clean billing boundaries.","risk":"Primary risk is in edge cases around tier changes during grace periods and the interaction between the 3-strike rule and 14-day grace period. Incorrect implementation could either cancel paying customers prematurely or allow indefinite free access.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Subscribers (Standard Tier)","impact":"Subject to 3-strike auto-cancellation; must create new subscriptions after cancellation."},{"group":"Subscribers (Premium Tier)","impact":"Receive 14-day grace period, providing more time to resolve payment issues before losing access."},{"group":"Free Trial Users","impact":"Limited to one concurrent trial per email, preventing trial abuse."},{"group":"Finance/Revenue Team","impact":"Clean subscription lifecycle ensures accurate revenue recognition and reduces bad debt from unbillable active subscriptions."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle complaints from customers who cannot reactivate cancelled subscriptions and must guide them through new subscription creation."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is well-defined with explicit rules and clear thresholds. Most rules are unambiguous. Confidence is not higher because a few interaction patterns between rules (e.g., tier downgrade during grace period, transient payment failures vs. hard declines) are not specified.","high_confidence_areas":["Payment method validation at activation (BR-1)","Terminal cancellation state (BR-4)","One-trial-per-email uniqueness constraint (BR-6)","Invariant classifications are well-grounded in state integrity vs. policy distinction"],"low_confidence_areas":["How tier changes during an active grace period affect which cancellation rule applies","Whether transient payment processor failures count toward the 3-strike limit","Whether the 7-day renewal window means exactly 7 days or 'at least 7 days before'","How the 14-day grace period interacts with the renewal retry schedule"],"assumptions":["The 3-strike rule for standard tier means 3 consecutive failed attempts within a single billing cycle's renewal window","Premium tier grace period replaces (not supplements) the 3-strike rule","An expired or completed free trial is no longer 'active' and does not block new trials","Payment method status is checked at the moment of activation, not at the moment of subscription creation"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
